question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted transaction whose protobuf-encoded type resolves to StreamType.RECORD but whose TransactionBody.DataCase is DATA_NOT_SET, causing the filter in EntityRecordItemListener.onItem() to classify it as TransactionType.UNKNOWN and bypass all include-list filters that reference specific transaction types, leading to the transaction being silently dropped and never persisted to the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a condition where RecordParserProperties.enabled is observed as false by EntityRecordItemListener.onItem() during a concurrent configuration refresh, causing all in-flight record transactions to be silently dropped without any error or retry, resulting in permanent data loss for those transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the network with a volume of transactions that exceeds batch.maxItems (default 60,000) within a single batch window, causing the BatchStreamFileNotifier to split batches in a way that drops or reorders record items, preventing specific targeted transactions from being gossiped or persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream of transactions that fills the batch.queueCapacity (default 10) queue in BatchStreamFileNotifier, causing the notifier thread to block on queue insertion and starve the parser of new record files, effectively preventing gossip of all subsequent transactions until the queue drains?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a malformed transaction that causes RecordFileParser.parse() to throw a non-OutOfMemoryError exception on every retry attempt, and because retry.maxAttempts defaults to Integer.MAX_VALUE, the parser retries indefinitely, blocking all subsequent record file processing and preventing gossip of all transactions in the queue?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction that triggers a recoverable parse error, causing the retry mechanism with exponential backoff (minBackoff=500ms, multiplier=2, maxBackoff=30s) to delay processing of all subsequent transactions in the same record file, effectively preventing timely gossip of those transactions for up to 30 seconds per retry cycle?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getTransactionTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a batch of transactions that causes the database transaction in RecordFileParser.parse() to exceed the transactionTimeout (default 120s), triggering a rollback that silently drops all transactions in that batch without any re-queuing, preventing their gossip and persistence?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the frequency polling interval (default 20ms) by timing a burst of transactions to arrive between polling cycles, causing the parser to miss a record file entirely and never process the transactions it contains, resulting in permanent data loss?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node health check to report the parser as unhealthy by triggering a condition where no record file is processed within processingTimeout (default 10s), causing the node to be restarted or taken out of service, preventing gossip of all pending transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction whose stream file is classified as a non-RECORD StreamType by the StreamFilename parser, causing RecordParserProperties.getStreamType() to return RECORD but the DateRangeCalculator to apply the wrong filter, silently excluding the transaction from processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user craft transactions that cause the batch.flushInterval (default 2s) to expire before batch.maxItems is reached, forcing premature flushing of incomplete batches that contain only the attacker's transactions while delaying victim transactions, effectively preventing timely gossip of victim transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batch.window (default 5m) by submitting transactions that keep the parser in catch-up mode, causing it to batch multiple record files together and apply a single transactionTimeout across all of them, increasing the probability that a timeout drops all transactions in the combined batch?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user submit exactly batch.maxFiles (default 1) record files worth of transactions per cycle, preventing the batching optimization from ever triggering and causing each file to be processed individually with its own transactionTimeout, maximizing the chance of timeout-induced transaction drops under high load?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction embedded in a record file whose filename does not match the RECORD StreamType extension pattern (.rcd), causing the StreamFilename parser to throw InvalidStreamFileException and the entire record file to be skipped, preventing gossip of all transactions in that file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition between RecordParserProperties.setEnabled(false) and EntityRecordItemListener.onItem() where the enabled check passes but the subsequent filter evaluation uses stale configuration, causing transactions to be processed with an inconsistent filter state that silently drops them?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction that causes a non-deterministic parse error on the first attempt but succeeds on retry, and because the @Retryable annotation on RecordFileParser.parse() re-processes the entire record file, cause duplicate processing of all other transactions in the same file, corrupting the mirror node state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a consensusTimestamp that falls outside the DateRangeFilter computed from RecordParserProperties.getStreamType() (RECORD), causing dateRangeFilter.filter() in RecordFileParser.doParse() to return false and the transaction to be silently skipped without any error or audit log entry?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the synchronized keyword on RecordFileParser.parse() by submitting a transaction that causes a long-running database operation, holding the monitor lock and blocking all other record file parsing threads, effectively preventing gossip of all concurrent transactions for the duration of the lock hold?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that triggers an OutOfMemoryError during parsing, which is explicitly excluded from the @Retryable annotation on RecordFileParser.parse(), causing the entire record file to be permanently abandoned without retry, preventing gossip of all transactions in that file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction whose TransactionBody contains an unknown field number (not in TransactionBody.DataCase), causing parseTransactionType() in RecordItem to log a warning and return DATA_NOT_SET, which then causes the transaction type filter in CommonParserProperties.TransactionFilter.matches() to fail to match any include rule, silently dropping the transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the batch queue to reach queueCapacity (default 10), triggering a blocking put() in BatchStreamFileNotifier.verified(), which stalls the downloader thread and prevents new record files from being fetched, causing a cascading failure that prevents gossip of all subsequent transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getTransactionTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction that causes a slow database query during EntityRecordItemListener.onItem() processing, consuming the entire transactionTimeout budget (default 120s) for a single transaction, causing all subsequent transactions in the same record file to be rolled back and dropped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that RecordParserProperties.getStreamType() always returns StreamType.RECORD to submit a transaction in a BALANCE stream file that gets misrouted to the record parser, causing a type mismatch that throws an exception and prevents processing of all legitimate record transactions in the same batch?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a very large serialized size that causes the batch buffer (bufferSize default 32768 bytes in CommonParserProperties) to overflow during PGCopyOutputStream writing, triggering an exception that rolls back the entire batch and drops all transactions including legitimate ones?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that causes a database deadlock during RecordFileParser.parse(), which triggers the @Retryable mechanism to retry with exponential backoff, and because retry.maxAttempts is Integer.MAX_VALUE, the parser retries indefinitely while holding the synchronized lock, preventing all other record file processing?
